publications.title: Divergent T-cell receptor recognition modes of a HLA-I restricted extended tumour-associated peptide.
publications.authors: Chan KF, Gully BS, Gras S, Beringer DX, Kjer-Nielsen L, Cebon J, McCluskey J, Chen W, Rossjohn J.
publications.executive_summary: Two structurally distinct TCRs derived from a polyclonal T-cell repertoire recognize HLA-B*07:02 presenting a 13-amino-acid tumor-associated peptide (NY-ESO-1₆₀₋₇₂), despite HLA-I molecules typically binding only 8–10 amino acid peptides. The TCRs employ divergent binding modes, with differential engagement of the extended peptide and variable conformational changes at the TCR-pMHC interface. This work demonstrates that flexibility at the TCR-pHLA-I interface enables polyclonal recognition of extended tumor epitopes, clarifying how T cells can tolerate structural variation while targeting cancer-associated antigens and informing strategies for TCR engineering in neoantigen-directed immunotherapy.
